target.name: Caerulomycins A and K
target.motivation: Caerulomycins A and K are members of a family of aromatic alkaloids possessing antimicrobial, immunosuppressive, and cytotoxic properties. The authors aim to demonstrate the utility of their new pyridine synthesis methodology in the total synthesis of these natural products.

prior_syntheses.key_strategy: selective, multiple C-H functionalizations of pyridines

success_criteria.free_text: Achieve total synthesis of caerulomycins A and K in only five steps from TosMIC, showcasing the utility of the developed heterocyclization methodology.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-C bond between pyridine and bipyridine/phenyl group
key_disconnections.reasoning: To introduce the second pyridine ring (for Caerulomycin A) or the phenyl moiety (for Caerulomycin K) at the 2-position of the pyridine ring, starting from an iodinated pyridine precursor.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-C bond of the oxime group
key_disconnections.reasoning: To install the oxime functionality from a methyl group, via oxidation to an aldehyde followed by reaction with hydroxylamine.
The core pyridine ring is formed via a novel tandem heterocyclization of an alpha-allylic TosMIC derivative with an electrophile (specifically, an iodinating agent for the synthesis of 6k).

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The strategy leverages a newly developed heterocyclization of alpha-allylic TosMIC derivatives to efficiently construct the substituted pyridine core. Subsequent functionalization steps (cross-coupling, oxidation, oxime formation) are then applied to rapidly access the target natural products. This approach is designed to be concise and operationally simple, demonstrating the power of the new pyridine synthesis method.

thought.background: TosMIC (1) is a versatile building block. The authors aim to synthesize alpha-allylic alpha-alkyl TosMIC derivatives (2a-2m) as precursors for their new pyridine synthesis.
thought.gap: Efficiently synthesize a range of alpha-allylic alpha-alkyl TosMIC derivatives with varying substituents.
thought.decision: To synthesize alpha-allylic alpha-alkyl TosMIC derivatives, chose a one-pot phase-transfer catalyzed (PTC) process, expecting good to excellent yields and suppression of disubstituted byproducts by sequential addition of alkyl halides.

outcome.observation: Isonitriles 2a-2m were obtained in good to excellent yields (51-89%). The initial step involved the addition of one equivalent of a simple alkyl halide at 0 °C, to suppress the formation of disubstituted byproducts. This was followed by the addition of substituted allylic bromides.

evidence.text_span: First, synthesis of suitable TosMIC derivatives was a prerequisite for exploring our new approach. The target $\alpha \cdot$ - allylic $\alpha$ -alkyl compounds $\left( 2 \mathbf { a } - 2 \mathbf { m } \right)$ were prepared via a single one-pot phase-transfer catalyzed (PTC) process. TosMIC reagent 1 reacted sequentially with two different alkyl halides in a biphasic system $\mathrm { [ C H _ { 2 } C l _ { 2 } / N a O H }$ $( 4 0 \% )$ ] using tetrabutylammonium iodide (TBAI) as the phase-transfer catalyst. The initial step involved the addition of one equivalent of a simple alkyl halide at $0 ^ { \circ } \mathrm { C } ,$ to suppress the formation of disubstituted byproducts. This was followed by the addition of substituted allylic bromides. The process furnished isonitriles ${ \bf 2 } { \bf a } { - \bf 2 } { \bf m }$ in good to excellent yields (Table 1).
evidence.section: Synthesis of Multisubstituted Pyridines by Heterocyclization of TosMIC Derivatives

thought.background: Alpha-allylic TosMIC derivatives (2) are available. The goal is to form pyridine derivatives via heterocyclization using carbonyl electrophiles, exploiting the dual reactivity of isocyanides.
thought.gap: Identify optimal conditions (solvent, Lewis acid, equivalents) for the heterocyclization with carbonyl compounds.
thought.decision: To access pyridine derivatives, chose to react alpha-allylic TosMIC 2a with acetone and AlEt2Cl, expecting cyclization through a tandem process involving alkene addition and nucleophilic attack of the isocyanide.

outcome.observation: The reaction of 2a with acetone and AlEt2Cl in CH2Cl2 gave 3a in 76% yield. This condition was found to be optimal after screening various solvents and reagent equivalents.

evidence.text_span: Based on our previous experience with heterocyclizations of TosMIC derivatives, 10b,c we selected a carbonyl electrophile whose reactivity was enhanced by the addition of a Lewis acid, for our initial studies. Thus, $\alpha$ -allylic TosMIC 2a was treated with acetone and $\mathrm { A l E t } _ { 2 } \mathrm { Cl }$ . Various solvents and reagent equivalents were screened, with the best results obtained using 2 equiv of both reagents in $\mathrm { C H _ { 2 } C l _ { 2 } }$ (Table 2).
evidence.section: Synthesis of Multisubstituted Pyridines by Heterocyclization of TosMIC Derivatives

thought.background: Optimal conditions for heterocyclization with carbonyl compounds have been established (2 equiv AlEt2Cl in CH2Cl2).
thought.gap: Evaluate the scope of this cyclization with various alpha-allylic TosMIC derivatives and different carbonyl compounds.
thought.decision: To study the scope, applied the best conditions (AlEt2Cl in CH2Cl2) to a series of alpha-allylic TosMIC derivatives (2a-2m) and five different carbonyl compounds (aldehydes and ketones), expecting to obtain corresponding pyridine products (3a-3o).

outcome.observation: Pyridine products 3a-3o were obtained in moderate to excellent yields (42-94%). The substituent at the 4-position (R2) of the target pyridine played a key role, with aromatic substituents generally affording higher yields due to carbocation stabilization.

evidence.text_span: Best conditions found for TosMIC derivative 2a were applied to study the scope of this cyclization. A series of $\alpha$ - allylic TosMIC derivatives $\left( 2 \mathbf { a } - 2 \mathbf { m } \right)$ was reacted with five different carbonyl compounds, including two aldehydes and three ketones of both aliphatic and aromatic types (Scheme 1). The corresponding pyridine products 3a−3o were obtained in moderate to excellent yields $( 4 2 - 9 4 \% )$ ).
evidence.section: Synthesis of Multisubstituted Pyridines by Heterocyclization of TosMIC Derivatives

thought.background: The heterocyclization works well with carbonyl electrophiles. The authors want to expand the scope to other electrophiles.
thought.gap: Determine if electrophilic CF3 reagents, epoxides, or imines can participate in the heterocyclization.
thought.decision: To evaluate the scope, attempted heterocyclization of 2a with electrophilic CF3 reagents, propylene oxide, N-tosyl aziridine, or Eschenmoser’s salt in the presence of AlEt2Cl, expecting cyclization.

outcome.observation: No cyclization occurred with any electrophilic CF3 reagent tested, nor with propylene oxide, N-tosyl aziridine, or Eschenmoser’s salt in the presence of AlEt2Cl.

evidence.text_span: To further evaluate the scope of this new pyridine synthesis, we explored the heterocyclization of $\alpha$ -allylic TosMIC derivative 2a using other types of electrophiles. The results were varied: no cyclization occurred with any electrophilic $\mathrm { C F } _ { 3 }$ reagent tested,14 nor with propylene oxide, N-tosyl aziridine, or Eschenmoser’s salt in the presence of $\mathrm { { A l E t } } _ { 2 } { \mathrm { C l } }$ .
evidence.section: Synthesis of Multisubstituted Pyridines by Heterocyclization of TosMIC Derivatives

thought.background: Previous attempts with various electrophiles failed. Michael acceptors might be more suitable.
thought.gap: Determine if Michael acceptors can serve as electrophiles in the heterocyclization and if the substituent effect at the 4-position persists.
thought.decision: To expand the scope, chose to employ the Michael acceptor ethyl acrylate instead of carbonyl compounds under the same reaction conditions, expecting cyclization with reactive substrates.

outcome.observation: Moderate yields were obtained when ethyl acrylate was employed, but cyclization occurred only with the most reactive substrates (those bearing an aryl group at the 4-position of the pyridine ring), yielding 4a (37%) and 4b (57%).

evidence.text_span: However, moderate yields were obtained when the Michael acceptor ethyl acrylate was employed instead of the carbonyl compounds under the same reaction conditions, although cyclization occurred only with the most reactive substrates—those bearing an aryl group at the 4-position of the pyridine ring (Scheme 1, compounds 4a and 4b).
evidence.section: Synthesis of Multisubstituted Pyridines by Heterocyclization of TosMIC Derivatives

thought.background: Lewis acid-mediated cyclization with AlEt2Cl worked for carbonyls and Michael acceptors. The authors want to explore other acid catalysts.
thought.gap: Identify effective Brønsted or Lewis acids for the cyclization of 2a to pyridine 5a.
thought.decision: To investigate acid-mediated cyclization, treated TosMIC derivative 2a with catalytic amounts of various Brønsted and Lewis acids (CF3CO2H, TsH, AlCl3, BCl3, Yb(OTf)3) in CH2Cl2, expecting to find a suitable catalyst.

outcome.observation: CF3CO2H, TsH, and BCl3 led to decomposition. AlCl3 resulted in only starting material recovery. Yb(OTf)3 gave 37% yield, which was not optimal.

evidence.text_span: Acid-mediated cyclization was also investigated using both Brønsted and Lewis acids (Table 3). Thus, TosMIC derivative 2a was treated with catalytic amounts of various acids, and only $\csc ( \mathrm { O T f } ) _ { 3 }$ was found to promote the formation of pyridine 5a in good yield. Variation of the solvent and acid loading led to optimized conditions $\left( \mathrm { C H } _ { 2 } \mathrm { Cl } _ { 2 } , 0 . 3 \right.$ equiv of $\mathrm { S c } ( \mathrm { O T f } ) _ { 3 } )$ , which were subsequently applied to the synthesis of compounds 5b− 5d starting from precursors 2c, 2i and 2e, respectively (Scheme 3).
evidence.section: Synthesis of Multisubstituted Pyridines by Heterocyclization of TosMIC Derivatives

thought.background: Previous attempts with various acids for cyclization of 2a were unsuccessful or gave low yields. Sc(OTf)3 showed promise.
thought.gap: Optimize conditions for Sc(OTf)3-catalyzed cyclization and apply it to other substrates.
thought.decision: To achieve good yields, chose Sc(OTf)3 as the catalyst and optimized solvent and acid loading, finding CH2Cl2 with 0.3 equiv Sc(OTf)3 to be optimal. Applied these conditions to precursors 2c, 2i, and 2e, expecting to obtain pyridines 5b-5d in good yields.

outcome.observation: Sc(OTf)3 promoted the formation of pyridine 5a in 70% yield. Applying optimized conditions to precursors 2c, 2i, and 2e yielded 5b (78%), 5c (90%), and 5d (58%), respectively. The substituent at the 4-position (R2) again played a key role in determining the yield.

evidence.text_span: Thus, TosMIC derivative 2a was treated with catalytic amounts of various acids, and only $\csc ( \mathrm { O T f } ) _ { 3 }$ was found to promote the formation of pyridine 5a in good yield. Variation of the solvent and acid loading led to optimized conditions $\left( \mathrm { C H } _ { 2 } \mathrm { Cl } _ { 2 } , 0 . 3 \right.$ equiv of $\mathrm { S c } ( \mathrm { O T f } ) _ { 3 } )$ , which were subsequently applied to the synthesis of compounds 5b− 5d starting from precursors 2c, 2i and 2e, respectively (Scheme 3). Once again, the substituent at the 4-position $\left( \mathbb { R } ^ { 2 } \right)$ of the target pyridine was found to play a key role in determining the reaction yield, which ranged from $5 8 \%$ to $9 0 \%$ depending on the ability of the substituent at that position to stabilize the carbocationic intermediate.
evidence.section: Synthesis of Multisubstituted Pyridines by Heterocyclization of TosMIC Derivatives

thought.background: Metal-catalyzed cyclizations have been successful. The authors want to explore metal-free conditions.
thought.gap: Identify a suitable metal-free electrophile for the heterocyclization of 2a to pyridine 6a.
thought.decision: To perform the transformation under metal-free conditions, treated TosMIC derivative 2a with various iodinating agents (N-iodophthalimide, 1,3-diiodo-5,5-dimethylhydantoin, iodine) in CH2Cl2, expecting to find an effective reagent.

outcome.observation: N-iodophthalimide gave 65% yield, 1,3-diiodo-5,5-dimethylhydantoin gave 22% yield, and I2 gave 41% yield. While cyclization occurred, these yields were not optimal.

evidence.text_span: For this purpose, TosMIC derivative 2a was treated with various iodinating agents (N-iodophthalimide, 1,3-diiodo-5,5- dimethylhydantoin, iodine and $N$ -iodosuccinimide). Although all of them afforded the desired pyridine 6a (Table 4), NIS gave the best result. Subsequent optimization of the solvent and the amount of reagent led to the final conditions used to study the scope of this transformation (Scheme 4). It is worth noting that in this case, the reaction performs better when $\mathrm { C H } _ { 3 } \mathrm { C N }$ is used as the solvent.
evidence.section: Synthesis of Multisubstituted Pyridines by Heterocyclization of TosMIC Derivatives

thought.background: Previous attempts with various iodinating agents for metal-free cyclization of 2a showed NIS as the most promising, but conditions needed optimization.
thought.gap: Optimize solvent and NIS loading for the metal-free heterocyclization to maximize yield of 6a.
thought.decision: To achieve optimal yield, chose NIS as the iodinating agent and optimized solvent and reagent amount, finding CH3CN as solvent and 2.0 equiv of NIS to be the best conditions, expecting higher yields.

outcome.observation: NIS gave the best result (71% yield) in CH3CN. This optimized condition was then used to study the scope of the transformation.

evidence.text_span: Although all of them afforded the desired pyridine 6a (Table 4), NIS gave the best result. Subsequent optimization of the solvent and the amount of reagent led to the final conditions used to study the scope of this transformation (Scheme 4). It is worth noting that in this case, the reaction performs better when $\mathrm { C H } _ { 3 } \mathrm { C N }$ is used as the solvent.
evidence.section: Synthesis of Multisubstituted Pyridines by Heterocyclization of TosMIC Derivatives

thought.background: Optimized metal-free heterocyclization conditions using NIS in CH3CN are established. The substituent effect at the 4-position is known to be important.
thought.gap: Evaluate the scope of this metal-free heterocyclization with various alpha-allylic TosMIC derivatives and other halogens.
thought.decision: To study the scope, applied the optimized conditions (NIS in CH3CN) to a series of TosMIC derivatives (2a-2m) and also explored NBS/NCS for bromination/chlorination, expecting to obtain corresponding halogenated pyridines (6a-6m).

outcome.observation: The scope followed the same reactivity pattern, with aromatic substituents at the 4-position affording higher yields. Pyridine 6k was obtained in moderate yield by reducing reaction time. Brominated (6l) and chlorinated (6m) products were also obtained in moderate to good yields. A control experiment with BHT showed 6a in 49% yield, supporting the mechanistic hypothesis.

evidence.text_span: The scope of this heterocyclization using N-iodosuccinimide as the electrophile follows the same reactivity pattern as the previous transformations. Once again, the substituent at the 4- position of the pyridine ring strongly influences the efficiency of the process. In general, aromatic substituents afford higher yields than aliphatic ones, with the best result observed for the 4-MeO-Ph group. Notably, the reaction proceeds with moderate yield when starting from TosMIC derivative 2k, provided that the reaction time is reduced, affording pyridine 6k. The cyclization process was also shown to occur with other halogens (Br, Cl), employing NBS or NCS instead of NIS, which led to the formation of the corresponding products 6l and 6m in moderate to good yields. In addition, a control experiment supporting the Scheme 2 mechanistic hypothesis showed that treating 2a with 2 equiv of NIS and 1 equiv of the radical inhibitor 2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-methylphenol (BHT) provided 6a in $4 9 \%$ yield.
evidence.section: Synthesis of Multisubstituted Pyridines by Heterocyclization of TosMIC Derivatives

thought.background: Pyridine 6k (iodinated) is a key intermediate. Caerulomycin A requires a bipyridine motif.
thought.gap: Introduce the second pyridine ring at the 2-position of 6k.
thought.decision: To synthesize Caerulomycin A, chose a palladium-catalyzed Negishi coupling to substitute the iodine atom of 6k with a second pyridine ring, expecting compound 7.

outcome.observation: Compound 7 was obtained in 82% yield via Negishi coupling.

evidence.text_span: Regarding the coupling step, substitution of the iodine atom to install a second pyridine ring via a palladium-catalyzed Negishi coupling provided compound 7
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of Caerulomycins A and K

thought.background: Pyridine 6k (iodinated) is a key intermediate. Caerulomycin K requires a phenyl group.
thought.gap: Introduce a phenyl group at the 2-position of 6k.
thought.decision: To synthesize Caerulomycin K, chose a Suzuki reaction to introduce the phenyl group to 6k, expecting compound 8.

outcome.observation: Compound 8 was obtained in 90% yield via Suzuki coupling.

evidence.text_span: while a Suzuki reaction enabled the introduction of the phenyl group to yield compound 8.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of Caerulomycins A and K

thought.background: Compounds 7 (for Caerulomycin A) and 8 (for Caerulomycin K) have the desired pyridine core and substituent at the 2-position, but still have a methyl group at the 6-position.
thought.gap: Convert the methyl group to an oxime functionality.
thought.decision: To complete the synthesis, chose to oxidize the methyl group to an aldehyde using SeO2, followed by oxime formation upon treatment with hydroxylamine, expecting to obtain Caerulomycins A (9) and K (10).

outcome.observation: Caerulomycin K was obtained in good yield (50% over two steps) by an analogous route. Caerulomycin A had been previously synthesized from compound 7, implying successful conversion to the oxime.
outcome.free_text: Caerulomycin K was obtained in good yield by an analogous route, affording both natural products in only five steps from TosMIC.

evidence.text_span: As a result, the synthetic routes are analogous: a cross coupling reaction to introduce either the second pyridine ring or the phenyl moiety, followed by oxidation of the methyl group to an aldehyde using $S { \mathrm { e O } } _ { 2 } ,$ and final oxime formation upon treatment with hydroxylamine. Regarding the coupling step, substitution of the iodine atom to install a second pyridine ring via a palladium-catalyzed Negishi coupling provided compound 7, while a Suzuki reaction enabled the introduction of the phenyl group to yield compound 8. Caerulomycin A had been previously synthesized from compound $7 , ^ { 1 1 \mathrm { b } }$ whereas caerulomycin K was obtained in good yield by an analogous route, affording both natural products in only five steps from TosMIC.
evidence.section: Total Synthesis of Caerulomycins A and K

Overall Yield.interpretation: The paper states 'Caerulomycin K was obtained in good yield by an analogous route, affording both natural products in only five steps from TosMIC.' The yield for the final two steps to Caerulomycin K is 50%.

Longest Linear Steps.interpretation: The total synthesis of Caerulomycins A and K was achieved in five steps from TosMIC.

Total Steps.interpretation: The total synthesis of Caerulomycins A and K was achieved in five steps from TosMIC.

Failed Attempts Count.interpretation: Two explicit failed attempts were reported in the paper (step 4 and step 6).

validation.comparison_to_prior: The synthesis of Caerulomycins A and K was achieved in only five steps from TosMIC, which is a concise route. Caerulomycin A had been previously synthesized from compound 7, but the overall route from TosMIC is new.
validation.significance: The developed methodology provides a versatile and operationally simple method for constructing highly functionalized pyridines, leveraging the dual reactivity of isocyanides. Its utility is demonstrated by the concise total synthesis of biologically active natural products, Caerulomycins A and K, in a short linear sequence.

source.title: Synthesis of Multisubstituted Pyridines by Heterocyclization of TosMIC Derivatives: Total Synthesis of Caerulomycins A and K.
source.authors: García-García JA, Aceña JL, García-García P, Sucunza D.
